title: Fix homepage: critical views label, light mode contrast, mobile hero, welcome banner, CTA naming, empty continue reading
description:
There are SIX issues to fix in this file:

### Issue 1: 'Critical Views' stat shows 49 but isn't labeled clearly
The homepage shows a stat of 49 'Critical Views' but there's no explanation of what qualifies as a 'critical view' vs. the 28 papers in the 'Skeptical Analysis' domain. Add a brief tooltip, subtitle, or parenthetical clarifying what this number represents.

### Issue 2: Light mode 'Paper of the Day' has very poor contrast
In light mode ([data-theme='light']), the Paper of the Day section's heading and subtitle are nearly invisible against the light grey background. Add light-mode CSS overrides to ensure readable contrast (dark text on light background).

### Issue 3: Mobile hero title breaks mid-word
On mobile (375px width), the 'Psi Research Library' hero title breaks mid-word as 'Psi Researc / h Library'. Fix with responsive font sizing, word-break rules, or reducing the title font-size at small breakpoints.

### Issue 4: Welcome banner consumes excessive mobile viewport
On mobile, the dismissable welcome banner takes up most of the visible screen, pushing actual content below the fold. Reduce its size on mobile — shorter text, smaller padding, or collapse it to a more compact format at small breakpoints.

### Issue 5: Homepage CTA button says 'Methodology' but page is '/method' titled 'Library Analytics'
The homepage CTA button text should be consistent with the actual page. Change 'Methodology' to 'Method' or 'Library Analytics' to match the destination page, and ensure the href is '/method'.

### Issue 6: 'Continue Reading' section is always empty for new visitors
The Continue Reading section shows a 'Clear reading history' button but no papers, since it depends on localStorage that is empty for new visitors. Hide this entire section when there are no items in reading history, rather than showing an empty section with a useless clear button. Use JavaScript to check localStorage and only show the section if there are items.

Do NOT output any thinking or explanation. Output ONLY search/replace blocks.

title: Fix catalog: blinding filter shows 74% as 'Other / Unknown'
description:
On the catalog page, the 'Blinding' filter shows 263 of 354 papers (74%) as 'Other / Unknown', making the filter essentially useless. This is a data completeness issue, but the UX can be improved: either hide the 'Other / Unknown' category from the filter dropdown since it's not useful for filtering, or rename it to something clearer like 'Not Reported', or add a note explaining that blinding status is not available for most papers.

Do NOT output any thinking or explanation. Output ONLY search/replace blocks.
